label: minimum-distance repulsion to prevent collapse
notes: Bodies were collapsing toward origin over time — gravitational attraction + damping drained orbital energy and pulled motes into a tight cluster. Added Lennard-Jones-style close-range repulsion: when true (unsoftened) inter-body distance r < min_dist, applies F_rep = repulsion_k * ((min_dist/r)^4 - 1) along the separation vector, pushing bodies apart. Power 4 makes repulsion overwhelm gravity's softened 1/r^2 attraction at close range; equilibrium separation sits near min_dist. Two new ctrl_motes channels: min_dist (0.18), repulsion_k (0.40). Also includes cleanup of a partial bloom-restructure attempt: created blur_inner/level_inner/blur_outer/level_outer ops, then aborted after discovering compositeTOP only has 2 input slots (not 3 as I assumed); destroyed those 4 ops, restored comp_bloom input[1] to level_bloom, and reverted sphere radius from 0.012 → 0.025. Bloom restructure remains TODO — needs chained 2-input composites instead.

operations.post_state: destroyed in same move (failed bloom attempt)

operations.before_summary: physics with weak 1/r linear repulsion that gravity overwhelmed at close range — bodies collapsed
operations.after_summary: Lennard-Jones-style (min_dist/r)^4 - 1 repulsion using true (unsoftened) r — overwhelms gravity below min_dist, equilibrium at r=min_dist
